title: Anno sama Was Openly Trolling
wikitext:
[]: Anno-sama Was Openly Trolling             Quote Post by Tabby-chan » Thu Aug 13, 2015 2:31 am So I'm pretty much 100% certain someone's already picked up on this, so feel free to delete this topic if that's the case. But otherwise, did ANYONE else notice this? http://www.evangelion.co.jp/news_20150401.html This [s:3ulv341z]shit[/s:3ulv341z] was posted on April Fool's Day. Granted, I'm not sure the Japanese even recognize that holiday (holly jolly holidays), but I'm wondering if he's actually going to make a Gojira movie. I mean, I've heard more news indicating that he's actually going through with it and that it's already in pre-or-production, but the idea seems so far-fetched as it is (think about it--Gojira? Really? Why not write something new?), and I just can't accept that Anno-sama would actually go through with it. Taking a break from Rebuild to do a new project? Sure. But Gojira? Why? Perhaps we gave this too much hype when we heard it and he decided to actually do it because people flipped their [s:3ulv341z]shit[/s:3ulv341z] enough. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OX2VaVtFYfs ccc-p.bandcamp.com soundcloud.com/ccc-p

[]: Re: Anno-sama Was Openly Trolling             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Aug 13, 2015 3:20 am Tabby-chan wrote: Why not write something new?

[]: Re: Anno-sama Was Openly Trolling             Quote Post by Tabby-chan » Thu Aug 13, 2015 3:26 am pwhodges wrote: You think he always has? Let's see - Kare Kano was an adaptation of a manga; Cutie Honey and Re: Cutie Honey are two adaptations of existing property. The Giant God Warrior Appears in Tokyo short that came with Q has a base in past material. And although Rebuild is based on his own property, it's still based on a reworking of existing material. Making a new version of something that already exists is not strange to him.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Aug 13, 2015 3:56 am The "Anno trollolol" meme has to die. Several times in a row, each more brutal than the last. This topic's title is bad, and Tabby-chan should feel bad for making it. :p

[]:             Quote Post by Tabby-chan » Thu Aug 13, 2015 4:21 am Reichu wrote: The "Anno trollolol" meme has to die. Several times in a row, each more brutal than the last. This topic's title is bad, and Tabby-chan should feel bad for making it. :p

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Aug 13, 2015 7:16 am April 1st is the start of the Fiscal Year . If a major company like Toho has an announcement to make about one of its biggest properties it's the perfect day for that.

[]: Re: Anno-sama Was Openly Trolling             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Aug 13, 2015 7:51 am Tabby-chan wrote: A part of me wishes he WOULD write something original, though he has the green thumb for making other peoples' stories great.

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Thu Aug 13, 2015 8:02 am Hey, i liked that movie

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Aug 13, 2015 8:12 am Probably you'll like Anno's version a lot better when it comes out - I'm certainly looking forward to see what he makes.

[]:             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Thu Aug 13, 2015 8:26 am Oh there's no doubt of that. Gareth Edwards is good (i REALLY liked his previous film Monsters), but Anno is a freaking MASTER

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Thu Aug 13, 2015 8:35 am It's not far-fetched at all. Anno's a huge toku fan and Godzilla is a cultural icon over there. For him, this is probably a huge honor. Like if one of us got selected to direct a new Eva movie!

[]: Re: Anno-sama Was Openly Trolling             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 13, 2015 8:38 am UrsusArctos wrote: Not every original thing is great and not every adaptation is bad. Take a look at, say, Akira Kurosawa. Several of his movies were original screenplays that were highly regarded - The Seven Samurai being a classic case in point - but some of his adaptations were absolutely spectacular, such as Ran , which combines King Lear and the tale of warlord Mori Motonari. In fact, if Anno's doing Gojira, I wouldn't mind waiting longer for 4.0 because knowing Anno, it'll be one hell of a Godzilla movie (and a lot better than the crappy one from last year).

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Aug 13, 2015 10:24 am I'll repeat what I've said elsewhere - Anyone who thinks Anno's artwork is him "trolling" needs to go punch themselves in the face. Hard. If need be I'll be happy to assist in the punching in the face department.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Aug 13, 2015 11:20 am Tabby-chan wrote: Tabby-chan feels bad for even bringing it up, because I fear that we took a joke so seriously that he ended up going through with it.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Aug 13, 2015 1:29 pm Tabby-chan wrote: ... I fear that we took a joke so seriously that he ended up going through with it. We may have ENABLED...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Aug 13, 2015 2:23 pm ^ Yes, this. When Anno keeps his personal meaning of EoTV Episode 25 a secret from the public, and encourages others to find their own meaning in the rest of Evangelion, we're not affecting him. He's affecting us. Our claiming that he's "trolling" or that we can "enable" his narrative and creative tendencies in ways other than monetary exchange is simply us trying to push back or even deny exactly how Anno affects us.

[]: Re: Anno-sama Was Openly Trolling             Quote Post by Adaminator1 » Thu Aug 13, 2015 11:07 pm Tabby-chan wrote: This [s]shit[/s] was posted on April Fool's Day.

[]:             Quote Post by Shoujo Kakumei Asuka » Fri Aug 14, 2015 4:48 pm Anno loves Tokatsu so there's no reason that he wouldn't make one. Besides, that would be like the type of "April Fools" prank that Squidward played on Spongebob, for no real reason. The movie will come out, and will likely be one of the better ones, although also likely not the best. (People tend to REALLY underestimate the wealth of talent that's been poured into Godzilla, and I think Anno's somewhat more comfortable in animation than live action from what I've seen) Gendo'sPapa wrote: I'll repeat what I've said elsewhere - Anyone who thinks Anno's artwork is him "trolling" needs to go punch themselves in the face. Hard. If need be I'll be happy to assist in the punching in the face department.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 14, 2015 8:54 pm Shoujo Kakumei Asuka wrote: That's still getting a bit over the top though. A stupid opinion isn't a reason to get that mad at someone. It's not like he said he was a pedophile and planned to act on it (I realize you were likely partially joking).

[]: Re: Anno-sama Was Openly Trolling             Quote Post by PainXIII12 » Wed Sep 30, 2015 8:19 pm Tabby-chan wrote: So I'm pretty much 100% certain someone's already picked up on this, so feel free to delete this topic if that's the case. But otherwise, did ANYONE else notice this? http://www.evangelion.co.jp/news_20150401.html This [s]shit[/s] was posted on April Fool's Day. Granted, I'm not sure the Japanese even recognize that holiday (holly jolly holidays), but I'm wondering if he's actually going to make a Gojira movie. I mean, I've heard more news indicating that he's actually going through with it and that it's already in pre-or-production, but the idea seems so far-fetched as it is (think about it--Gojira? Really? Why not write something new?), and I just can't accept that Anno-sama would actually go through with it. Taking a break from Rebuild to do a new project? Sure. But Gojira? Why? Perhaps we gave this too much hype when we heard it and he decided to actually do it because people flipped their [s]shit[/s] enough. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OX2VaVtFYfs ccc-p.bandcamp.com soundcloud.com/ccc-p
